name: brain-offline-fallback
description: Brain class implements 3-tier offline fallback: Full (BM25+vec+GraphRAG), Degraded (BM25+GraphRAG), Minimal (BM25-only). _try_enable_vector() loads sqlite-vec extension then model2vec; self.vector_enabled gates all vec paths.

name: rrf-fusion-pattern
description: reciprocal_rank_fusion() fuses N result lists: score[doc_id] += 1/(k+rank) for each list. Docs appearing in multiple lists get boosted scores. k=60 default. Always include BM25 list; add vector/graph lists when available.
brain_bootstrap() only indexed docs/ and core-steps/; if neither existed sources=[] and Brain was empty
resolution: Always add common src dirs (src/lib/scripts/plugins/hooks) and cwd fallback; always call ingest()

name: brain-stop-hook-fallback-reindex
description: prism_stop_hook conductor fallback: call Brain().incremental_reindex() before build_agent_instruction() when Conductor unavailable so Brain knowledge stays current

name: brain-corrupt-error-detection
description: Brain.__init__ wraps PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL in try/except sqlite3.DatabaseError → raises BrainCorruptError with db path label. _check_db_integrity() runs PRAGMA integrity_check as secondary guard. brain_bootstrap() catches BrainCorruptError, deletes .prism/brain/*.db, retries Brain() once.

FTS5 external content table (content='docs') requires AFTER INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE triggers on the content table. Without them: (1) INSERT OR REPLACE desynchronizes FTS5 silently, (2) manual DELETE FROM docs_fts WHERE id=? is wrong syntax for content tables.
resolution: Add docs_fts_ai/ad/au triggers in _init_brain_schema() executescript; remove manual FTS5 ops from _ingest_single() and _remove_entries_by_source(). Triggers handle all sync automatically.

name: brain-deps-actionable-messaging
description: _try_enable_vector() uses informative degraded mode message ('running in BM25+GraphRAG mode') instead of alarming 'unavailable'; __main__ init/ingest attempts pip install of sqlite-vec model2vec before Brain(); _cmd_init/_cmd_status print mode label (Full/BM25+GraphRAG).

name: brain-exclude-path-segments
description: Brain._should_index() uses _EXCLUDED_PATH_SEGMENTS set (.claude, .prism, __pycache__, node_modules, .git, .venv, venv, .env, dist, build, .tox, .mypy_cache, .pytest_cache, .overstory) with Path.parts membership check to skip state/cache dirs before suffix check.

name: brain-passive-vs-active-usage
description: Issue #8: Brain context appears passively injected but not actively queried. Root causes already fixed: path exclusions (3.0.6) prevent indexing state files, gate blocking (3.0.7) ensures workflow stops properly. In fresh/simple projects, Brain naturally has minimal useful content — this is expected.

name: brain-purge-deleted-cleanup
description: Brain _purge_deleted() method: queries all docs with source_file, removes entries where file no longer exists or fails _should_index(). Called at end of ingest() and incremental_reindex(). incremental_reindex uses --diff-filter=ACMRD (added D) and separately handles deleted files.

name: brain-relevance-threshold
description: Brain.system_context() filters search results by rrf_score >= 0.1 threshold before including in context. Results below threshold are excluded. last_result_count reflects post-filter count.
Brain relevance threshold 0.1 was mathematically unreachable with RRF k=60. Max possible: 2/61=0.033 (2 lists) or 3/61=0.049 (3 lists). Caused bq=0 on all steps despite Brain being active.
resolution: Lowered threshold to 0.02. Always validate thresholds against the scoring function output range.

name: vector-domain-filter
description: sqlite-vec vec0 domain filter: _vector_search() cannot use WHERE on non-vec columns. Fix: over-fetch limit*3 when domain set, then post-filter by joining doc_id to docs table (SELECT id FROM docs WHERE id IN (...) AND domain = ?). Cap final results at original limit.

name: sqlite-conn-execute-readonly
description: sqlite3.Connection.execute is read-only in Python — cannot be monkeypatched directly. To intercept SQL calls in tests, replace the entire connection attribute (e.g. brain._brain) with a wrapper class that has its own execute() method delegating to the real connection for non-intercepted queries.

name: auto-bootstrap-search
description: Brain.search() auto-bootstraps: if docs table has 0 rows, calls ingest(_cli_source_dirs()) before searching. Also calls incremental_reindex() on non-empty searches (may need throttling).

name: mulch-expertise-ingestion
description: Brain._ingest_mulch_expertise() reads .mulch/expertise/*.jsonl, indexes each record as domain=expertise with doc_id=expertise:{domain_name}:{record_id}. Called from ingest().

name: vector-domain-post-filter
description: Brain._vector_search() post-filters domain: over-fetches 3x, joins doc_id back to docs table for domain check, returns filtered[:limit]. sqlite-vec vec0 doesn't support WHERE on non-vec columns.

name: brain-cli-new-commands
description: _cmd_graph(brain, entity) prints entity relationships from brain.graph_query(); _cmd_explain(brain, file) shows indexed chunks and graph entities via direct DB queries; _cmd_rebuild(brain) calls _purge_deleted() then ingest(). All dispatched in __main__.

name: overstory-logs-source-file-none
description: _ingest_overstory_logs() uses source_file=None (not the real path) because .overstory is in _EXCLUDED_PATH_SEGMENTS — passing the real path causes _purge_deleted() to immediately remove the just-indexed entries. Uses doc_id='sessions:<rel_path>' for content-hash deduplication.

name: brain-analytics-cmd
description: _cmd_analytics() reads outcomes.jsonl from .prism/brain/ directory, groups by (persona, step_id), shows avg/best/worst scores and last 10 recent outcomes. Dispatch via 'analytics' command in brain_engine.py __main__ block.

name: ast-chunking-implemented
description: Brain indexes files as semantic chunks (function/class/module/imports) using tree-sitter with regex fallback. Doc IDs: {filepath}::{entity_name}. Docstring extraction for embedding-friendly summaries.

name: session-start-hook
description: session-start.py: SessionStart hook runs Brain incremental_reindex, seeds .prism/brain/memory/MEMORY.md (Slavka pointer pattern), prints systemMessage with doc count.

name: brain-conductor-stderr-diagnostics
description: 5 except blocks in conductor_engine.py and prism_stop_hook.py now log specific error type+message to stderr instead of silently swallowing. Fallback logic preserved.

name: fts5-rrf-threshold-test-pattern
description: FTS5-graph RRF fusion threshold: Brain._fts5_search uses implicit AND semantics (all query tokens must appear in doc). story_file query tokens must be present verbatim in indexed content for FTS5 to match. When doc_id==source_file, BM25+graph both return same doc_id, fusing to RRF=2/61≈0.033>0.02 threshold. Avoid extra words in story queries absent from indexed content.

name: conductor-try-init-brain-stderr-test
description: To test Conductor._try_init_brain() stderr output: patch brain_engine.Brain with side_effect before Conductor() instantiation. The 'from brain_engine import Brain' inside _try_init_brain() looks up Brain from the already-imported module, so patching the module attribute works. Use capsys to capture stderr.

name: brain-context-test-coverage
description: test_brain_context_and_diagnostics.py: 13 tests covering system_context() output (non-empty with docs, empty without, tag format, last_result_count), Conductor stderr diagnostics (Brain unavailable message, exception propagation, _brain_available flag), stop_hook fallback message format, and end-to-end build_agent_instruction with/without brain_context.

name: brain-auto-install-vector-deps
description: _auto_install_vector_deps(): one-time pip install guard for sqlite-vec + model2vec, called from _try_enable_vector() on ImportError. Uses _VECTOR_INSTALL_ATTEMPTED flag + importlib.invalidate_caches() post-install so retry imports resolve.

name: brain-exclude-user-sentinel
description: Brain._load_user_excludes() reads .prism/brain/exclude (one path segment per line, # comments) to let teams add project-specific exclusions without code changes. _should_index() checks this after _EXCLUDED_PATH_SEGMENTS.

name: auto-install-vector-deps
description: _auto_install_vector_deps() helper in brain_engine.py: attempts pip install sqlite-vec model2vec on ImportError in _try_enable_vector(), uses importlib.invalidate_caches() after install, retries imports. No sentinel file yet (follow-up).

name: bm25-exclude-user-config
description: _EXCLUDED_PATH_SEGMENTS now includes sample-resumes, samples, sample-data, test-data, testdata, fixtures, seed-data, mock-data. Also supports .prism/brain/exclude file (one pattern per line) for user-specified exclusions via fnmatch in _should_index().

name: hf-hub-warning-suppression
description: brain_engine.py suppresses HF Hub unauthenticated-request warning at module level using os.environ.setdefault('HF_HUB_DISABLE_IMPLICIT_TOKEN','1') and warnings.filterwarnings('ignore', message=r'.*unauthenticated requests.*HF Hub.*'). _load_model2vec() also wraps model loading in warnings.catch_warnings for belt-and-suspenders coverage.

name: brain-sources-allowlist
description: Brain._ALLOWED_SUBPATHS frozenset controls which subpaths within _EXCLUDED_PATH_SEGMENTS are still indexed. _should_index() checks allowlist first (normalized / separators) before applying exclusions. Enables .claude/skills/ and .prism/brain/memory/ to be indexed.

name: cli-source-dirs-expanded
description: _cli_source_dirs() now covers: docs/, src/, lib/, scripts/, plugins/, hooks/, plugin core-steps, root *.md files, .claude/skills/, .prism/brain/memory/, .prism/handoff.md, auto-detected dirs (app/, packages/, modules/). Fixes GitHub #19 where brain indexed only 42 docs on 2000+ file project.

Mixing 'with patch(module.Path)' and monkeypatch.setattr() on same attribute corrupts module state after test. The with-block exit restores to original, but monkeypatch teardown restores to the MagicMock (its saved value).
resolution: Use only monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path) for cwd-dependent functions. Never mix patch() context manager with monkeypatch on same attribute.

name: rebuild-walks-cwd
description: _cmd_rebuild now calls brain.ingest([str(Path.cwd())]) instead of _cli_source_dirs(). This ensures manually-ingested dirs survive a rebuild, since ingest() walks the entire tree and _should_index() handles exclusions.

name: windows-utf8-encoding
description: Windows cp1252 console UnicodeEncodeError fix: call stream.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') on sys.stdout and sys.stderr at script entry points. Works in Python 3.7+ since TextIOWrapper.reconfigure is available.

content: _EXCLUDED_PATH_SEGMENTS includes bin, obj, .playwright, storybook-static to prevent build artifact dirs from polluting Brain search results.

name: brain-rebuild-full-walk
description: _cmd_rebuild() now walks entire project root instead of _cli_source_dirs(), so rebuild doesn't reset to hardcoded dirs. _should_index() handles exclusions.

name: windows-utf8-reconfigure
description: brain_engine.py and prism-bug.py use sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace') at entry point to fix Windows cp1252 UnicodeEncodeError.

name: ingest-os-walk-pruning
description: Brain.ingest() uses os.walk(topdown=True) with dirnames[:] pruning for _effective_excludes. Allowed subpath parents (.claude, .prism) are kept for descent. onerror=lambda _: None handles corrupted NTFS dirs. Replaces p.rglob('*') which descended into excluded dirs before _should_index check.

name: rebuild-no-upfront-purge
description: Brain._cmd_rebuild() must NOT call _purge_deleted() before ingest(). If ingest crashes mid-walk, an upfront purge leaves 0 docs. ingest() already calls _purge_deleted() after successful traversal at line 1915.

name: fts5-implicit-and-hazard
description: FTS5 implicit-AND hazard: passing long text (100+ tokens) as FTS5 MATCH query requires ALL tokens to appear in a single document — virtually never matches. Fix: extract top 10-15 key terms (stopword-filtered) and join with OR syntax. Implemented _extract_fts_query() in brain_engine.py.

name: rrf-threshold-single-index
description: RRF threshold 0.02 is mathematically impossible with k=60 when only one search index contributes: max score = 1/(60+1) = 0.0164 < 0.02. Fix: adaptive threshold — 0.02 when vector enabled (3 lists), else 1/(k+1)*0.9 ≈ 0.0148 (safely below single-list max). Implemented in system_context() in brain_engine.py.

content: Brain.record_skill_usage() now accepts step_id param (default ''). Per-step queries via get_skill_scores_for_step(step_id). Schema migration via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with try/except. Old callers without step_id still work.

system_context() FTS5 fix: pre-extract terms via _extract_fts_query(raw, max_terms=20) from story file (2000 chars) instead of passing raw markdown; add fallback returning top-N when RRF threshold filters all results — prevents 0-result Brain queries when FTS5-only installs have low RRF scores

Brain.system_context() accepts step_id param: when provided, augments FTS5 query with _STEP_QUERY_KEYWORDS[step_id] via OR so consecutive steps (draft_story, verify_plan) get differentiated results even with identical story files.

Brain.system_context() deduplication: _previous_context_doc_ids set tracks last call's returned doc_ids; on next call, deduped results exclude them. Search uses limit*2 headroom when dedup is active to ensure alternatives are available. Falls back to full results if dedup would leave nothing.

Brain.seed_skill_usage(skills) pre-populates skill_usage table with sentinel session_id '__seed__'; maps skill priority to canonical phase step (build→implement_tasks, verify→write_failing_tests, top_level→draft_story); called from Conductor.select_relevant_skills when global skill_usage is empty and all_skills is non-empty for cold-start bootstrapping
